title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
description:
An array of distinct integers, originally sorted ascending, has been rotated at some unknown pivot (e.g. `[0,1,2,4,5,6,7]` could become `[4,5,6,7,0,1,2]`). Given the rotated array `nums` and a `target`, return the index of `target` in `nums`, or `-1` if it isn't present. Your solution should run in O(log n).

**Example**

```
nums = [4, 5, 6, 7, 0, 1, 2], target = 0  ->  4
nums = [4, 5, 6, 7, 0, 1, 2], target = 3  ->  -1
```
hints: At any midpoint, at least one of the two halves (left..mid or mid..right) is guaranteed to be normally sorted.
Figure out which half is sorted, then check whether target lies in that half's range to decide which side to recurse into.

solutions.name: Modified binary search
solutions.explanation: Run binary search, but at each step determine which of the two halves around mid is contiguously sorted by comparing nums[left] to nums[mid]. If the left half is sorted and target falls within nums[left]..nums[mid], search left; otherwise search right. Symmetrically for a sorted right half. This discards half the array each iteration despite the rotation.
